node_proposed.name: Nicol Bolas
node_proposed.frontmatter_pattern_basis: cards/_artists/jenn-ravenna-tran.md (mirrors the artist layer's skeleton: type / name / aliases / universe / canonical_source / confidence / appears_on / related_hubs)

description: Sir, Edgelord steps aside and Mr. Nodeley apologizes for the inconvenience. Three trivia-passed cards depict adjacent narrative beats of one named character's defeat (Bolas-Falls cluster: Topple the Statue Act 2 + Despark Act 3 + Prison Realm Act 3 coda), all carry Scryfall story_spotlight: true, all are designer-confirmed for the same set's spotlight cycle, and the load-bearing connection across them is the character himself, not an iconographic primitive or a single visual motif — Topple shows his statue, Despark shows his slave-mage commanding his Eternals against him, Prison Realm shows him in person bound under Ugin's containment. The right shape is a character node, which also means proposing the characters layer (more sniveling). The existing Despark↔Topple 1:1 edge stays as a card-to-card cohesion mirror; the new character node accrues future Bolas printings (Nicol Bolas Dragon-God, Bolas's Citadel, the Amonkhet block, the Conflux block) cleanly without requiring N²/2 mirror edges. I hope this is acceptable.
citations: [Scryfall: story_spotlight: true on WAR-26, WAR-35, WAR-190]
[MTG Wiki: Meditation Plane — confirms post-WAR rename to Prison Realm under Ugin's containment]
[MTG Wiki: War of the Spark — confirms 27-card story-spotlight cycle and Bolas-Falls cluster]
[War of the Spark: Ravnica novel, Greg Weisman, Del Rey 2019 — canonical prose source for the defeat sequence]
files_modified: cards/_characters/nicol-bolas.md (CREATED — also introduces the cards/_characters/ directory)

alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Hubs are foundational brand-anchor concepts hand-curated by Alex (Labor, Rebellion, Chinese Zodiac per memory bbl-hubs-hand-curated). Bolas-Falls is a specific 3-card narrative event inside a single set, not a brand-anchor. Wrong layer.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Symbol at cards/_symbols/fallen-idol.md (or similar — toppled-monumental-sculpture-of-tyrant iconography)
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: The three cards do not share a single iconographic primitive — Topple has a literal collapsing dragon-god statue, Despark has broken god-statues as background behind Liliana, Prison Realm has Bolas in person bound in a magenta void. The load-bearing connection is the character, not the iconography. A future fallen-idol symbol could legitimately edge to Topple specifically, but it would not unify the cluster.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Broader hub at cards/_hubs/story-spotlight.md (all Scryfall story-spotlights across all sets)
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Metadata-tier, not narrative-tier. story_spotlight is a Scryfall flag — useful as a search predicate, but not the kind of foundational thesis-concept that anchors a Discrete Lair. The 27-card WAR cycle alone wouldn't make a coherent bundle; the Bolas-Falls subset would. The narrative subset is where the gravity lives, and the character anchor captures it.

alternatives_considered.candidate: DARK NODESLEY EX dissolution of the existing Despark↔Topple 1:1 edge (subsumed by character node?)
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Considered and rejected. The Despark↔Topple 1:1 edge has its own load-bearing content (paired story-spotlight Acts of the same arc, designer-tagged, mirrored bidirectionally) and remains valid card-to-card cohesion data even with the character node in place. It is not subsumed in the symbol-replaced-1:1 sense; it complements. The character node anchors the cluster; the 1:1 mirror records the act-pairing. Both stay. No dissolution needed.

future_passes_unlocked: Future Bolas-depicting cards (Nicol Bolas Dragon-God WAR-207, Nicol Bolas Planeswalker, Bolas's Citadel WAR-79, Liliana Dreadhorde General WAR-97 as agent-of-defeat-edges-to-Bolas) attach to this node directly without N²/2 mirror edges.
A future Ugin character node would edge to Prison Realm as the jailer side of the same beat (twin/counterposition relation between Nicol Bolas and Ugin).

A future Liliana character node would edge to Despark as the protagonist-of-the-act side; Despark would carry edges to both Bolas (object of defeat) and Liliana (agent of defeat) — character nodes legitimately co-occupy a card.

The Bolas-Falls cluster is now a bundle-cohesion stack. Three cards converging on one thematic anchor is a 'stack' topology per the edgelord spec; the bundler will love this. A Discrete Lair on Bolas's defeat could be authored from these three cards plus future Bolas-arc additions, with the why_it_fits prose anchored to the character node's canonical content.

layer_proposal_notes.recommendation: wikilintbot.py line 187 should be extended to include "character" in the type tuple of _is_non_card_node, so character MDs are properly excluded from inventory checks even if a future one is placed outside _characters/. The current _-prefix directory rule already covers everything under cards/_characters/, so this is a forward-compatibility recommendation rather than a blocker.

frontmatter_schema.ip_resolution_for: Optional field: when a character is the canonical resolution for one or more cards' suspected_ip frontmatter value, list the IP string here. Mirrors the artist layer's aliases-driven name resolution pattern. Enables a future triviabot pass to validate that suspected_ip values across the corpus resolve to a character node.
